gb
Design System v2 · Golfballs.com Extension

The system

Token-driven foundation that already works in light or dark. Every primitive below reads only from --gb-* variables — no literal rgba colors anywhere.

00

Audit of the current theme system

Before we can ship a light theme — or any consistent visual change — the codebase needs to graduate from literal rgba() to semantic tokens.

What works today
  • ~30 --gb-* tokens covering brand, surfaces, borders, text, error/success/warning, page-injected buttons
  • Runtime override via chrome.storage.themeColors + broadcast
  • Same names available in both theme.js (content scripts) and theme.css (popup/editor)
What blocks a light theme
  • ~200 instances of literal rgba(255,255,255,.0X) as fills, hovers, borders, scrollbars
  • ~50 instances of rgba(0,0,0,.X) for recess effects and footer strips
  • No tokens for: shadows, focus ring, backdrop, scrollbar, fill overlays, on-brand text, status text-on-fill
  • Brand tints written as rgba(var(--gb-brand-label-rgb), .15) in 40+ places — verbose and impossible to swap
01

Tokens

Five semantic groups. The 'fill' group is the new addition that makes light theme possible — it auto-inverts based on the active theme.

Surface · page & card backgrounds, layered
Concrete colors. Goes from "deep" (page edges, header strips) up through "floating" (popovers).
deep
--gb-surface-deep
canvas
--gb-surface-canvas
surface 1
--gb-surface-1
surface 2
--gb-surface-2
surface 3
--gb-surface-3
modal
--gb-surface-modal
Fill · the new layer that makes theming work
Semi-transparent overlays. In dark mode they're white-tinted, in light mode they're black-tinted — same token, opposite color. Replaces every literal rgba(255,255,255,.0X).
faint
--gb-fill-faint
subtle
--gb-fill-subtle
soft
--gb-fill-soft
medium
--gb-fill-medium
strong
--gb-fill-strong
Inverse fills · darken on dark, lighten on light
inv-soft
--gb-fill-inverse-soft
inv-medium
--gb-fill-inverse-medium
inv-strong
--gb-fill-inverse-strong
Border
subtle
--gb-border-subtle
default
--gb-border-default
strong
--gb-border-strong
Text · by role
Primary
--gb-text-primary
Secondary
--gb-text-secondary
Tertiary
--gb-text-tertiary
Muted
--gb-text-muted
Ghost
--gb-text-ghost
On brand
--gb-text-on-brand
Brand & status · 5-token shape per family
Every status (brand / error / warning / success) has the same five tokens: solid, fg (text color), tint-soft, tint-medium, tint-border. That's all you need for buttons, badges, callouts, focus states.
BRAND
solid
--gb-brand
fg
--gb-brand-fg
tint-soft
--gb-brand-tint-soft
tint-medium
--gb-brand-tint-medium
tint-border
--gb-brand-tint-border
ERROR
solid
--gb-error
fg
--gb-error-fg
tint-soft
--gb-error-tint-soft
tint-medium
--gb-error-tint-medium
tint-border
--gb-error-tint-border
WARNING
solid
--gb-warning
fg
--gb-warning-fg
tint-soft
--gb-warning-tint-soft
tint-medium
--gb-warning-tint-medium
tint-border
--gb-warning-tint-border
SUCCESS
solid
--gb-success
fg
--gb-success-fg
tint-soft
--gb-success-tint-soft
tint-medium
--gb-success-tint-medium
tint-border
--gb-success-tint-border
Note: --gb-brand-label is the same as --gb-brand-fg. It's the brand color you use for icon strokes, match dots, anchor text, anything that needs to read as "green" against any surface.

01b

Theming

A new theme is a list of 19 colors. Everything else — tints, focus rings, hovers, shadows — recomputes from those 19 automatically.

Click any preview to apply it to this whole page. Notice how the brand label, surfaces, and text shift — everything else (border opacities, status tints, focus rings, hover fills) recomputes automatically because it derives from the 19 base tokens.
ATOMIC · 19 tokens
What you define per theme
Concrete colors. Hand-picked. Pick these, and you have a theme.
Fill mode2
--gb-mode-fill-baseWhich way fills tint (e.g. "255,255,255")
--gb-mode-fill-inverseInverse direction for input recess
Surface6
--gb-surface-deepHeader/footer strips, page edges
--gb-surface-canvasPage background
--gb-surface-1Card / input background
--gb-surface-2Hover / selected card
--gb-surface-3Floating menu
--gb-surface-modalDropdowns and modal body
Text5
--gb-text-primaryHeadings
--gb-text-secondaryBody
--gb-text-tertiaryLabels
--gb-text-mutedHints
--gb-text-ghostPlaceholders
Border3
--gb-border-subtleLightest divider
--gb-border-defaultCard outline
--gb-border-strongEmphasized outline
Brand1
--gb-brand-labelThe one driving color. Every brand-tint-* derives from this.
Status4
--gb-errorError solid
--gb-warningWarning solid
--gb-successSuccess solid
--gb-infoInfo solid
DERIVED · ~50 tokens
What you get for free
Calculated once, applies to every theme. Uses color-mix() and rgba() against the atomic tokens.
5
All --gb-fill-*
derives from --gb-mode-fill-base · rgba()
3
All --gb-fill-inverse-*
derives from --gb-mode-fill-inverse · rgba()
4
All --gb-brand-tint-*
derives from --gb-brand-label · color-mix()
4
All --gb-error-tint-*
derives from --gb-error · color-mix()
4
All --gb-warning-tint-*
derives from --gb-warning · color-mix()
4
All --gb-success-tint-*
derives from --gb-success · color-mix()
4
All --gb-info-tint-*
derives from --gb-info · color-mix()
1
--gb-border-focus
derives from --gb-brand-label
1
--gb-text-on-tint
derives from --gb-brand-label
2
All shadows
derives from Static rgba (same both themes)
1
--gb-focus-ring
derives from --gb-brand-tint-medium
12
Radii, motion, type
derives from Global constants
Recipe · add a new theme19 LINES
Paste this block at the bottom of system-tokens.css, change the 19 values, then set document.documentElement.dataset.theme = "your-theme".
[data-theme="your-theme"] { /* Fill mode — 255,255,255 for dark-ish, 0,0,0 for light-ish */ --gb-mode-fill-base: 255, 255, 255; --gb-mode-fill-inverse: 0, 0, 0; /* Surfaces */ --gb-surface-deep: #0a0b0c; --gb-surface-canvas: #0e0f10; --gb-surface-1: #16181a; --gb-surface-2: #1c1f22; --gb-surface-3: #232629; --gb-surface-modal: #131517; /* Text */ --gb-text-primary: #f5f6f7; --gb-text-secondary: #d4d6d9; --gb-text-tertiary: #9ca0a6; --gb-text-muted: #6b6f76; --gb-text-ghost: #45494f; /* Borders */ --gb-border-subtle: #1a1c1f; --gb-border-default: #26292d; --gb-border-strong: #393d42; /* Brand & status */ --gb-brand-label: #8fce2e; --gb-error: #e25a5a; --gb-warning: #e0a030; --gb-success: #4ec48c; --gb-info: #6ab0f3; }
Rules of thumb
Want a shade variant of an existing theme?
Only swap the --gb-surface-* values. Keep everything else. That's how Midnight differs from Dark — it just goes deeper.
Want a whole new mood?
Swap surfaces + text together (they have to read against each other). Cream changes 11 values out of 19 — surfaces, text, borders, and one brand shade for legibility.
Don't override the derived tokens
If you find yourself adding --gb-brand-tint-medium to a theme block, stop. Either your --gb-brand-label is wrong for the theme, or you need a new component variant.
Test in seconds
Wire your settings panel to call document.documentElement.dataset.theme = id and persist it in chrome.storage.local.themeColors.theme. No reload required.
02

Button

One <Btn> component with six variants × four sizes. The tinted variant accepts a status prop so the green 'Charge' button and the red 'Refund' button share the same code path.

Variants
Sizes · xs / sm / md / lg
States
Where each variant lives in your code
primary
.btn-send · .btn-save · .wl-btn-confirm · .gb-btn-primary · sp-btn-submit
secondary
.btn-charge (idle) · .btn-order-edit · .btn-watch-add · .gb-btn-close · .hdr-manage · .upc-btn
tinted (brand)
.btn-charge.ready · sb-tab.active · .preset-btn.active
tinted (error)
.btn-charge.refund · .btn-watch-show.has-critical · .ccm-flag-chip.junk
ghost
.btn-icon · .gb-rte-btn · .btn-back · .tpl-item:hover (variant)
danger
.btn-del · .btn-reset-all · .upc-btn.danger
dashed
.btn-sb-new (+ New Template) · .btn-add
03

Icon button

Square buttons that hold just an icon. Most-used variant is the close button at the top-right of every modal — but also row-delete actions, header settings gear, RTE toolbar buttons.

Tags, chips, dots

Three small label primitives. Tags are uppercase status badges. Chips are mixed-case (variables, filters). Dots are 6–10px filled circles, optional glow for match indicators.

Form controls

Single visual shape across Input · Textarea · Dropdown. All share inputBaseStyle() — they recess into the surface using --gb-fill-inverse-medium, so they look right against any background.

05b

Callouts, checkboxes, sliders

Three more primitives I missed on the first pass: callouts (5 tones, replaces .note-callout), real checkboxes (for list/table selection, distinct from the switch), and slider bars.

Callout · 5 tones · same left-border accent as .note-callout
Smart Triggers
This template activates when its match rules pass on an order page. Use {{var}} tokens to inject DOM-extracted data.
Case Reply Template
Matches against the From / Subject / Body of incoming case emails. Variables are extracted by regex from those same fields.
Saved
Template synced across all open tabs.
Heads up
Conditions are evaluated against the contact's live Solr record. Leave empty to always match.
Charge failed
The processor returned insufficient_funds. Suggest the customer update their card before retrying.
No icon. No title. Just a quiet hint sitting at the bottom of a form.
Checkbox · distinct from switch

Switch-tag · label + state in one control
For inline feature toggles and per-row enable controls. The tag carries the label, the embedded switch carries the state. Replaces the awkward "label + separate switch" pattern in the settings panel.

06

Modal · header / body / footer

Every injected modal in the extension follows the same three-zone pattern. The header always gets a 30px icon tile, title (13px/700), subtitle (11px/muted), optional right-side meta, and a close IconBtn. The footer always lives over an inverse-fill recess.

Subtitle context · meta

Three-zone contract
Header--gb-fill-inverse-strong
30px icon tile + title/subtitle + right meta + close
13/700 title · 11/500 subtitle
Body--gb-surface-canvas
Scrollable. Sections via <SectionLabel>. Forms in 2-col / 3-col grid.
12/500 body · 9.5/700 uppercase labels
Footer--gb-fill-inverse-strong
Hint · Cancel · Primary (right-aligned). Optional Destroy at far left.
Btn md · 32px tall
06b

Notification ideas

Six structurally different directions. Each preview has its own Fire button (re-plays the entrance animation) and a side picker where it makes sense.

APill
Radical minimalism
Single line. Dot · message · close. No icon tile, no label. Fits content width. Use for passive confirmations.
When to use · Auto-save · synced · copied
Side
preview · click Fire to re-play animation
Template saved
BAction card
Carries its own CTA
Card-shaped, not a strip. Primary + secondary actions baked in. Forces the toast to do work — Undo, Retry, View.
When to use · Deleted (Undo) · charge ready (Retry) · proof sent (View)
Side
preview · click Fire to re-play animation
Charge ready to run
3 cards on file totaling $1,247.50. Confirm to capture.
CStep tracker
Multi-step operations
Shows progress through named steps. Each step has done/active/pending state. Persists until done.
When to use · Submit proof · run campaign · sync templates · bulk export
2/4
preview · click Fire to re-play animation
Submitting proof…
Validating proof file0.8s
Uploading to S30.8s
Creating CRM entry…
Notifying art team
DNotification tray
Collapsed badge → expands on click
Stacks fade-in events into a single anchored badge with a counter. Click to expand the feed.
When to use · Watch List updates · background CRM sync · email auto-detection
Side
preview · click Fire to re-play animation
5 new
EEdge strip
Ambient, hangs from screen edge
Hangs down from the top edge of the viewport. Short, single-line, hidden until needed. Stays out of the way.
When to use · Background status · "dev mode active" · connection lost
Side
preview · click Fire to re-play animation
Watching 142 orders · last sync 12s ago
FFloating banner
Informative · mid-width · still floats
Sits between Pill (too small) and Edge (too wide). 480px max. Floats with a shadow, has icon + title + message + close. The default for substantive informative notifications.
When to use · Match found · feature toggled · template synced · campaign started
Side
preview · click Fire to re-play animation
Match found
3 templates match this order. Click to pick.
These six aren't mutually exclusive — a mature extension uses several. Pill for "copied to clipboard". Banner for "3 templates matched". Action card for "Charge ready · run". Step tracker for "Submitting proof…". Tray for the background event stream. Edge strip for "dev mode active".
06d

Feature toggle · attention-grabbing variants

When a feature flag isn't background config but a headline decision — the user is here to flip it, look at it, understand its impact. Five variants from compact to spotlight.

A · SPOTLIGHT
Large icon · soft halo when on
The default attention-grabbing variant. Big 44px icon tile, prominent switch, soft 4px halo glow when active. Use for primary feature toggles users explicitly seek out.
Charge Card
Show the Charge / Refund button in the toolbar popup. Captures payments from anywhere on the order page.
Auto-push proofsEXPERIMENTAL
Automatically submit proof requests when an order matches predefined rules.
B · HERO
Gradient background · radial glow · ACTIVE / OFF tag
Most dramatic. The card itself changes mood when toggled. For "headline" features at the top of a settings panel — Dev Mode, the main extension on/off, anything that defines the experience.
Dev ModeACTIVE
Pipes test data into every modal so you can preview the extension without touching real orders. Disables all live API calls.
C · PREVIEW
Toggle controls a live mini-preview
Bottom half shows exactly what the toggle controls — desaturated when off, full color when on. Removes ambiguity about "what does this do?" Use for visual features (button visibility, popup elements).
Templates in popup
Show the templates picker in the toolbar popup.
Preview · visible
Templates picker
D · BANK
Grouped toggles · master switch + mixed state
Master toggle at top, children below. Indicator shows "X/Y enabled" and warns when mixed. Replaces the existing "Popup Buttons" / "Page Enhancements" sections in your manage panel.
Popup buttons
4/6 enabled· mixed
Charge Card
Shows the Charge / Refund button in the popup.
Order Edit
Inline order edit modal on order pages.
Submit Proof
Submit Proof button on order/contact pages.
Watch List
Order follow-up timers and watch popup.
My Tasks
Personal task list with keyboard shortcut.
CRM Search
Searchable CRM modal across contacts/accounts.
E · STATUS
Toggle + live activity stats
When the feature has measurable state — open watches, queued tasks, pending matches — show it. Live/Idle dot tells the user the feature is actually doing work right now.
Watch ListLive
Order follow-up timers with live alerts.

Spotlight — primary controls users seek out
Hero — headline features that define the experience
Preview — visual features (button visibility)
Bank — grouped flags with a master
Status — features with live measurable activity

06e

Color spotlight · attention-grabbing color pickers

Color pickers built like the feature toggles — when a color choice deserves attention, not buried in a settings list. Same 5-variant shape: Spotlight · Hero · Preview · Bank · Status.

A · SPOTLIGHT
Full-height swatch · hex · reset
The 88px swatch is the click target. "EDITED" pill appears in the corner when modified. Halo glow shows the row is active.
Brand label--gb-brand-label
The primary green — match dots, focus rings, brand tints all derive from this.
B · HERO
The color IS the background
Most dramatic. Auto-flips text color for readability. Best for the single hero color of a theme — the one decision that defines everything else.
Theme accent
The flagship color. Changes everything tinted in the system.
--gb-brand-label
C · PREVIEW
Live preview of where the color shows up
Bottom panel renders example UI using the picked color. Edit it and watch the components change in real time. For colors with downstream visual impact.
Error / destructive--gb-error
Used for error toasts, validation, delete buttons, fraud alerts.
Live preview
CRITICAL
Charge failed
D · BANK
Grouped palette · stacked swatch preview · reset all
For coherent palettes — status colors, brand family, a section of related tokens. Header shows the palette at a glance with overlapping swatches.

Custom/Default state + contrast readout
Shows whether the color differs from the default, plus a live WCAG contrast ratio against the surface. Picks for accessibility-sensitive colors (text, button fg).
Brand textCustom
Used as the foreground on tinted brand backgrounds.
#BCE066--gb-brand-label

Spotlight — single attention-worthy color in a list
Hero — the theme's flagship color
Preview — colors with visible downstream impact

Status — accessibility-sensitive colors

06f

Expandable feature controls

Two dropdown-style feature controls from the settings panel: Direct Send via Power Automate (with flow URL config) and Developer Mode (with test console). Built on a shared ExpandableFeature primitive — toggle in header, sub-settings reveal below.

A · POWER AUTOMATE
Toggle + Flow URL config + setup helper
Warning-toned because misconfiguring this routes real emails through an unverified flow. The amber treatment + ACTIVE pill make the state unmistakable.
Direct Send via Power Automate ACTIVE
When enabled and a flow URL is set, the send button becomes Send and emails go directly through Power Automate — no Outlook window.
https://prod-22.eastus.logic.azure.com/workflows/8a7c…
Set up in Power Automate
  1. Create New flow → When an HTTP request is received
  2. Add a Send an email (V2) action
  3. Save and paste the generated URL above
Connected · last test 2m ago
B · DEVELOPER MODE
Toggle + test console with categorized actions
Brand-toned because this is a sanctioned tool, not a risky setting. The console groups actions by category (Notifications, Modals) with quick-fire buttons.
Developer Mode ACTIVE
Reveals a test console for firing notifications and opening modals on the active tab. API calls fail gracefully — UI is fully visible.
Notifications
Modals
API calls inside modals will fail gracefully — UI is fully visible.
Pattern notes
  • Header is the click target — clicking anywhere on the row toggles the feature. The switch is visual confirmation, not the only affordance.
  • Body slides in from the top with the bounce easing when the toggle activates — same motion vocabulary as toasts and modals.
  • Both variants share ExpandableFeature — only the tone prop differs (warning vs brand).
  • The body content is fully composable — anything you'd put in a settings card goes inside. Field controls, helper callouts, test buttons.
07

Migration plan

What it takes to switch the extension over. Phase 1 is non-disruptive — adopting tokens — and Phase 2 actually adopts React.

PHASE 1
Tokenize the existing CSS
Vanilla JS / HTML stays exactly as it is. We do a find/replace across all content/*.js files:
  • rgba(255,255,255,.05) → var(--gb-fill-subtle)
  • rgba(255,255,255,.08) → var(--gb-fill-soft)
  • rgba(0,0,0,.3) → var(--gb-fill-inverse-medium)
  • rgba(0,0,0,.4) → var(--gb-fill-inverse-strong)
  • rgba(var(--gb-brand-label-rgb), .15) → var(--gb-brand-tint-medium)
After Phase 1, light theme works — you flip document.documentElement.dataset.theme = 'light' and every surface adapts.
PHASE 2
Adopt React per surface
One modal at a time, replace the manual innerHTML generation with React. Suggested order — easiest first:
  1. popup.html (small, isolated, no iframe)
  2. charge.html (already its own document)
  3. Watchlist + Task List modals (simple list UIs)
  4. Margin Calculator + Submit Proof
  5. CRM Create Contact
  6. CRM Search + Query Builder (data-heavy)
  7. Campaign Builder (most complex)
  8. editor.html (full-tab manager) — last
Each modal becomes a single React tree mounted into a <div id="__gb-X-root">. The primitives in this page are the shared library — they live next to theme.js.
Toggle the theme at the top — every primitive on this page reads only from --gb-* tokens. That's the deal: anything you build with these stays consistent and themeable forever.
